steps.title: Compare to the target form
steps.content: The expression must equal $ry^4(15y-10)$. Factor the original expression until the part $y^4(15y-10)$ appears.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
First factor out $y^4$:
$$105y^5-70y^4=y^4(105y-70)$$
Then factor $7$ from the parentheses:
$$y^4(105y-70)=7y^4(15y-10)$$
So $r=7$.

steps.title: Check the result
steps.content: The factored form $7y^4(15y-10)$ expands back to $$7y^4\cdot15y-7y^4\cdot10=105y^5-70y^4.$$ So the constant multiplier is $7$.
